mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side constraints that compress short-term corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of sharp INDPRO decline events, or the signal shows no predictive power during periods of stable industrial output.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while core inflation remains elevated, the resulting real rate compression forces equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of cases where both conditions hold, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports long-term earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP signals an economic downturn that immediately reduces equity risk appetite, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of GDP contraction quarters, or the signal fails to predict returns when GDP growth is between 0.0% and 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment indicates an overheating economy that prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of overheating signal occurrences, or the joint condition shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.
